completed_tasks: Separated deterministic measurement from agent musical adjudication in the skill workflow and evidence contract.
Added portable ffmpeg and ffprobe discovery, explicit-path diagnostics, conversion failure handling, and a Python soundfile metadata fallback.
Added a measurement-only harmony evidence extractor that preserves tempo-level, beat-grid, key, source-specific chroma, bass, chord, and recurrence hypotheses.
Preserved frame timing independently for full-mix and harmonic sources so differently trimmed stems remain safe.
Emitted valid partial_evidence instead of evidence_ready when tempo, beat-grid, or segment measurements are incomplete.
Added structural validation that forbids final-decision fields and confidence inside measurement artifacts.
Added complexity restraint for unsupported seventh candidates and retained multiple candidates per segment.
Added unit tests for preprocessing errors, metrical ambiguity, grid hypotheses, recurrence similarity, extension restraint, partial evidence, evidence contracts, and final-analysis compatibility.
Exercised the extractor on the supplied recording and on intentionally mismatched full-mix and stem durations without feeding it the previous final chord chart.
Installed the refined repository skill into the active Codex skill directory and confirmed zero drift.
notes: The extractor writes measurement artifacts only and cannot write a final chord chart.
The supplied recording produced approximately 69.84 and 139.68 BPM hypotheses, each requiring agent selection.
The supplied recording preserved multiple key hypotheses including C minor and E-flat major rather than automatically selecting one.
A 20-second full mix and 19.5-second harmonic stem completed successfully with 12 segments and 12 non-authoritative recurrence descriptors.
